reviewer_notes: Full taint-flow trace and manual source audit across all 7 PHP files. All automated findings are false positives.

verdicts.reason: The taint source $_POST['resume_id'] is cast to (int) immediately at line 36, yielding a pure integer. That integer is then used in wpdb->prepare() with a %d placeholder — correctly parameterized. The array returned by get_col() is further walked with intval via array_walk($attachments, 'intval'). wp_send_json() encodes the final array as JSON with Content-Type application/json — there is no HTML rendering context, so integer values in a JSON response cannot produce XSS. The taint engine incorrectly propagated labels through (int) cast and prepare().

The taint source $_POST['id'] is cast to (int) immediately at line 67, yielding a pure integer. The integer is passed to imsanity_resize_from_id(), which immediately re-casts it with (int) again at line 338 of utils.php. The $results array is constructed entirely from: (a) translated strings wrapped in esc_html__(), (b) database-derived values (attachment metadata), and (c) integer IDs. wp_send_json($results) sends application/json — no HTML rendering context. No XSS is possible.

trace_verdict.source_sanitized_at: line 67 via (int) cast, re-cast in imsanity_resize_from_id() line 338

notable_non_issues.assessment: Not exploitable. Data comes from the plugin's own custom DB table (imsanity_ms). The only write path is imsanity_network_settings_update(), which requires manage_options capability + valid nonce. A superadmin can already execute arbitrary code via WP core. No injection path from untrusted input.
Not injectable. $wpdb->imsanity_ms is set to $wpdb->get_blog_prefix(0) . 'imsanity' at plugin load — a WordPress-internal value with no user input.
All AJAX actions are registered on wp_ajax_* only (not wp_ajax_nopriv_*). Every handler verifies both capability and nonce before acting. No unauthenticated attack surface.

notable_non_issues.pattern: unlink, rename, fopen on image files
notable_non_issues.assessment: All file paths are derived from WordPress attachment metadata (wp_get_attachment_metadata, get_attached_file) or from the WP upload directory API. No user-controlled path components reach file system operations without going through WP internals first.
